summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Mountain Building & Geological Structures
summary_sections.content_html:
Mountain Building (Orogeny)

Types of Mountains

  • Fold Mountains: Formed by compression at convergent boundaries (Himalayas, Appalachians).
  • Fault-Block Mountains: Formed by tensional forces lifting blocks along normal faults (Sierra Nevada, Tetons).
  • Volcanic Mountains: Built by eruptions (Mt. Rainier, Mt. Fuji).
  • Dome Mountains: Uplifted by magma pushing up without erupting (Black Hills).

Geological Structures

  • Anticline: Upward fold (arch). Syncline: Downward fold (trough).
  • Fault types: Normal (tension), reverse/thrust (compression), strike-slip (shear).
